title: angadenia sagrae
intro: Angadenia sagrae, also known as the night-blooming cereus or moonflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, characterized by its large, white flowers that bloom only at night and wilt before dawn. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Angadenia sagrae include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and insomnia, and improve sleep quality, making it a popular natural remedy for insomnia and other sleep disorders. Angadenia sagrae can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
